verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a secreted growth factor with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation. Alternative splicing generates isoforms (A165, A189) that bind heparan sulfate on cell surfaces and in extracellular matrix, but this is reversible, non-covalent, equilibrium-based binding to matrix components—not a stable cell-surface anchor of VEGFA itself. The protein lacks any mechanism for its own stable surface anchoring: no TM precursor stage, no documented covalent TM-partner tethering, no cell-state-induced ectosurface display, no tissue-restricted or dual-localization pool. VEGFA is recruited to pericellular and matrix locations via soluble-phase diffusion and equilibrium interactions with cell-surface receptors and proteoglycans, but the protein body does not stably reach the outer leaflet by its own biophysical mechanism. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic program targets VEGFA as a cell-surface antigen.
